results.model_patch:
diff --git a/requests/models.py b/requests/models.py
index fbb3c7e6..8b6dde9e 100644
--- a/requests/models.py
+++ b/requests/models.py
@@ -321,7 +321,7 @@ class PreparedRequest(RequestEncodingMixin, RequestHooksMixin):
         """Prepares the given HTTP method."""
         self.method = method
         if self.method is not None:
-            self.method = to_native_string(self.method.upper())
+            self.method = method.upper()
 
     def prepare_url(self, url, params):
         """Prepares the given HTTP URL."""

headless.summary: Implemented the minimal fix in `requests/models.py` within `PreparedRequest.prepare_method`: replaced `to_native_string(self.method.upper())` with `method.upper()`, resulting in direct assignment to uppercase method without unnecessary conversion. Verified the file parses by running `python3 -m py_compile requests/models.py` successfully (only a pre-existing SyntaxWarning unrelated to this change).
